name: Time Picker
summary: A named shallow preset over the abstract `temporal` core: a single time-of-day picker. It pins the Temporal Intent to presentation=media (a spatial clock) and granularity=point (one time), bound to the native <input type="time"> anchor. Like its date-picker sibling it adds no machinery of its own — clock rendering, locale-aware parse/format, and keyboard roving are the core's; the native time input stays the zero-JS keyboard fallback. (A linear presentation — a scroll wheel — is the common mobile variant; this preset pins media, and a linear member would pin presentation=linear over the same anchor.)
designDecisions: A preset is a dimension pin, not new machinery (#713 option C) — time-picker is the `temporal` core with presentation=media + granularity=point pinned over input[type=time]. The distinct native anchor (input[type=time], its own parse/format and value shape) is exactly the platform reason it is a named preset and not a prop on date-picker (the slider, with one anchor, needed no such members).
input[type=time] is the anchor and fallback; the clock surface is the media enhancement, with the native time input the keyboard-entry, form-associated, no-JS baseline (the slider precedent).

ariaApgKeyboard.usage: A time-of-day preset: native `<input type="time">` or a spatial clock surface.
